feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: The Gotham Years
entry.summary:
Author: x_starfry

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 25,879Chapters: 10/45Kudos: 135

Summary: Damian al Ghul was raised to be a weapon. Then Bruce Wayne brought him home to a manor full of vigilantes, unresolved trauma, and far too many people willing to love him anyway. A series of one-shots following Damian’s childhood — from terrifyingly tiny assassin baby to Gotham’s smallest Robin.   Featuring: accidental family bonding Bruce Wayne panic-parenting tiny League-trained behaviors Batfamily chaos emotional damage healing Alfred running the entire household and one very small child slowly realizing he is safe Updates whenever inspiration strikes. (very often)

entry.title: Do Aliens Dream Of Purple Orchid Skies
entry.summary:
Author: NotCassia

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,895Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 10

Summary: When he shifted, his weight settling in deeper—he realised two things. He wasn't on the floor. He was in his bed. Right underneath his bedsheets, cocooned in silk. And his body wasn't completely free of strain, not if the weight fully draped at his left side was real. He shifted again—a human weight. Drew in a breathe through lungs that no longer burned with desperation—spiced cinnamon. When stripped of his Kevlar and cleaned of the soot of his city and the scent of war paint, Bruce smelled like spiced cinnamon.   Clark opened his eyes with caution, some parts to avoid the unpleasant sting of light ruthlessly burning through his eyes. Others to ease the sting of disappointment if it so happened Clark was hallucinating again.   Bruce's cheek was pressed to Clark's shoulder, his head tucked beneath Clark's chin—and it made looking all the more difficult. A generous amount of war paint was still smudged on the skin around Bruce's eyes, some of it smeared on Clark's skin.   Or, Clark's debilitating survivors guilt VS his boyfriend whom he's borderline down horrendous for.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: Out on the Ocean, Sailing away, I can hardly wait to see you come of age, But I guess we'll both just have to be patient
entry.summary:
Author: Hades_the_god_of_the_underworld

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 10,963Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 329

Summary: Despite what most people thought, Percy’s first title wasn’t ‘son of Poseidon’ or ‘defeater of the minotaur’. No, his very first title was Perseus Odysseus Wayne, Prince of Gotham. Gotham’s press went wild the day he was born –partially, because his father was only eighteen when he had him. While Gotham obviously wasn’t very conservative, it was still quite the scandal. Especially given that Percy’s other parent was completely unknown. His face was plastered across every headline for three weeks and would be a frequent part of the news for many years to follow. Being the only child of Bruce Wayne, the sole Wayne family heir, was a very big deal. Not that Percy really cared. - - - Or in a universe where Bruce Wayne, a man with clear sight blessed by Nemesis, fell in love with Poseidon, who was being compelled to have a child by the fates, have a son together, Percy Jackson's life ends up just a little different. Not enough to change the timeline, but enough to change his childhood and eventually, his life post-war. Especially when he discovers his dead brother is alive and subsequently returns to Gotham.

entry.title: Celebrant
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 30,471Chapters: 6/?Kudos: 22

Summary: Seven months into cohabitation. Tungsten collar permanent. Restraining order served, criminal charges filed, relationship public for six. Neither of them has worked out what ordinary looks like when you've spent three years earning it. This is the story about having the Thing. Bruce Wayne sits in a boardroom with the collar visible above his shirt collar and presents two billion dollars in contracts on four hours of patrol sleep and watches twelve people decide what to do with what they're seeing. Clark Kent sits across from his supervisor and is asked, for the first time in three years, whether he actually wants what he's been building — or whether it's the place he goes to not need anything. Ra's al Ghul comes back from the dead with an old claim. Lex Luthor sits in a courtroom. Gerald Gardener has been hoping since Bruce was eight years old. Dick Grayson has opinions. Alfred pours the coffee first. The collar doesn't come off. The work doesn't stop. The rope is still hemp.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: I Used To Obsess Over Living, Now I Only Obsess Over You
entry.summary:
Author: l0s3rzclub

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 84,707Chapters: 12/?Kudos: 258

Summary: Clark, of course, knew that Bruce Wayne had been studying at Metropolis University. The billionaire was all anyone could talk about when he first got accepted. Except for Clark. He'd actually counted himself lucky to never run into the guy. From the sounds of it, Bruce was just another rich kid with a superiority complex. ______ OR: Enemies to Lovers College AU because it's so fucking good.

entry.title: I'm Back in the Fucking Building Again?!
entry.summary:
Author: The_Bluest_of_Blues

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 37,158Chapters: 9/?Kudos: 319

Summary: In a post apocalyptic future, Batman and Superman are left alone. Everyone else is dead. After they conclude that there is nothing left for them to save, they somehow find themselves waking up in their 14 year old bodies all the way back in 1984! Neither are apt to look a gift horse in the mouth.

entry.title: Two Heroes and a Baby
entry.summary:
Author: xxEmilee_Lunaxx

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 131,934Chapters: 37/?Kudos: 672

Summary: In this AU, 19-year-old Bruce Wayne adopts 4-year-old Dick Grayson after he is orphaned by a tragic event at Haly's Circus. Bruce is in over his head trying to juggle establishing himself as a responsible and valuable asset to Wayne Enterprises, beginning his vigilante work in Gotham as The Batman, and parenting a young child despite not knowing where to start. After struggling to get good press and constantly being seen as an irresponsible nepo baby, he sets up an interview at The Daily Planet. That's where he meets 19-year-old intern Clark Kent, who is eager to write a good story to begin his career as a journalist. After Clark agrees to keep an eye on Dick during his interview, Bruce realizes he has a knack for watching children, especially young Dick Grayson. Bruce asks Clark to come live with him at Wayne Manor on a 6 month assignment as Dick's nanny, and in return, will let Clark write the story of a lifetime--an expose on his life that no other reporter has even come close to. Clark reluctantly agrees, but is hiding a secret from Bruce--his life as the superhero, Superman! As the two begin their friendship and double lives as heroes, they learn valuable lessons in friendship, love, and parenting.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: A Study in Lace and Pearls
entry.summary:
Author: Murmeloni

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,759Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 144

Summary: After a rough breakup and receiving life changing news, Bruce retreats to his vacation home on Chincoteague Island. There, he finds a lot more than the peace he sought. Or: getting stalked by the friendly dolphin Mer after dumping your shitty boyfriend isn't as bad as it sounds. It might actually be kind of perfect.

entry.title: Erase Myself and Let Go
entry.summary:
Author: theFamousLivingDead

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,669Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 50

Summary: "I thought we could play a little game, baby," Holds out the pouch for Clark, who carefully reaches out his hand to dig into it. The moment he does, he flinches and retreats his hand. "It's okay," Bruce says, still holding out the pouch. A soft line of concern crosses Clark's face before he shakes it off and lets his hand slip into the pouch again. When he retreats, he's holding the kryptonite choker that Bruce had made for him a few months ago. Where worry had colored Clark's expression before, it is quickly replaced with excitement. "I fixed the clasps on the back," Bruce adds before Clark could get a little too excited about breaking the rules of their game again. "You won't be able to open it as easily this time." Or.. Bruce fixed Clark's Kryptonite choker so he can't be a little brat anymore. Sequel to this one-shot, but can be read on its own.

entry.title: The Door Left Open
entry.summary:
Author: xanthoszephyrus644

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,717Chapters: 6/184Kudos: 11

Summary: When a Luthor trap puts Batman in a room he cannot think his way out of, the Justice Leaguelearns there are older rooms still locked inside him. A story about the kind of damage recordsdo not hold, the people who refuse to look away, and what it costs Bruce Wayne to be cared foron purpose. Tags: Bruce/Clark slow burn; found family; trauma recovery; referenced past abuse;hurt/comfort; Justice League ensemble; Batfamily; consent; memory loss; mind control; non-graphic past sexual abuse references. Reading order note —Numbered chapters are concurrent threads - what other people were doing during the main sceneor what the villains were building while the family recovered. Interludes are quieter close-upsbetween beats. Read straight through; skipping interludes is ok but some will be highly impactful to plot as a heads up! Book one of two. This volume closes the door it opened on Bruce — the healing, the family, thelove — and leaves one other door standing open, because some lessons outlive the people whotaught them, and someone patient is still waiting on the other side.

Author: Chiyo_Fujimori

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,401Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 40

Summary: "Clark brought up unpredictable conversation matters. And it was an early evening, the sun just beneath the horizon and a soft umbre over the silhouette of buildings, when Clark asked a strange question during their watchtower shift. 'Do you ever wonder what it would be like to be normal?'" ----- A moment in which Clark chooses to be vulnerable with Bruce during a quiet watchtower shift. What else can Bruce do but hold Clark so he knows that Bruce will always catch him?

entry.title: Bring the Bird Home
entry.summary:
Author: dolcesher

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 36,692Chapters: 16/16Kudos: 216

Summary: There had been one universe they’d visited in which he and Clark were together. Married. But Bruce refused to think about it. Everything was easier that way.Except now stood a child that seemed to be from a universe much like that one, where he and Clark were not just together, but parents.An eleven year old Dick Grayson is mysteriously transported from a parallel universe where he was adopted by Bruce and Clark as a baby. While Bruce takes care of him, he examines his relationship with his sons, and a certain Kryptonian that won't leave him alone.

entry.title: A Courtship In Five Stages
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,121Chapters: 5/5Kudos: 48

Summary: Six years in the same house. Never once the word for it. When Clark begins a courtship rite his dead world left him only fragments of, Bruce does what Bruce does: he reads ahead. The vessels are ash. The grammar is incomplete. They have a kitchen, a wood, and four pillows to make it out of. A wedding in five stages, improvised from whatever's on the shelf.

entry.title: Cage of Roses
entry.summary:
Author: endofer

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,110Chapters: 1/14Kudos: 2

Summary: The story Clark had heard wasn't entirely clear and previous monster hunters who'd come investigating had been sent packing, unwilling to talk about it. Some details remained the same between retellings: there was a monstrous creature in this town, it punished crime and protected the town from invasion, and it occasionally asked for a human sacrifice.In a castle overlooking a town lives a monstrous bat creature. It is the town’s protector and a source of fear. Clark Kent hears about these rumours and comes to investigate. A retelling Beauty and the Beast with MonsterHunter!Clark and Beast!Bruce ft. Alfred and a handful of batkids.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
  • Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne
  • Clark Kent
  • Bruce Wayne
  • Alfred Pennyworth

  • But like in a hot way
  • or at least clark things so
  • self-hate
  • Body Dysmorphia

  • batman has wings
  • batkids have feathers
  • no beta we die like superheroes’ parents
  • minor dubcon in dream sequences

entry.title: After the Last Heartbeat
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 52,291Chapters: 10/?Kudos: 12

Summary: Alfred Pennyworth woke up dead at 11:34 AM on a Tuesday in October and by midnight had reorganized the suppression rotation, argued protocols with Jason Todd, held Thomas Wayne for the first time in forty years, and filed a math anomaly in the Naldoreth accounting under investigation required. After the Last Heartbeat is the fourth part of The House We Never Leave. It is the one where the dead side gets its full complement, where Alfred and Jason discover what Naldoreth actually is and what fixing it will require, where Bruce and Clark search the archives and all the lights go out and the crowd comes to the windows singing in Yoruba and Bantu and Swahili and Xhosa and doesn't leave at dawn, where Clark makes his decision about his door, and where Alfred learns fourteen names with hundreds still to go. The accounting has begun. It will take years. They are doing it anyway.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings
  • Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne
  • Clark Kent & Alfred Pennyworth & Bruce Wayne
  • Alfred Pennyworth/Martha Wayne/Thomas Wayne
